svg:
Ultra-Low Latency LRU Cache Architecture
Act 1: Fetch Data (Cache Hit)
Act 2: Fetch Data (Cache Miss - Early Exit)
Act 3: Store Data (Insert)
Act 4: Eviction (Capacity Reached)
Application Layer (Client)
Lock-Free Asynchronous Messaging
Zero-Allocation Core Engine
Application Thread Translates Strings to uint64_t
SPSC Inbox Queue O(1) Lock-Free Ring Buffer
SPSC Outbox Queue
Dedicated Cache Engine 100% Isolated Thread Execution
Flat Array Hash Map
Contiguous Memory Pool
Arbitrary Data Sources (Client Logic, Subsystems, etc.)
Returned Results Cache hits / Data reads
1. Push Request Stream
2. Consume Event
3. Hash Probe
4. Returns Index
5. O(1) Node Access
6. R/W Data
7. Enqueue Result
8. Poll Response Stream

\n
Hash(K1)
\n
Idx: 42
\n
Read[42]
\n
{User1}
\n
Res:{User1}
\n

\n

\n
Not Found
\n

\n

\n
Hash(K3)
\n
Ack: New
\n

\n
Ack: OK

\n

\n
Pop LRU
\n

\n
